pages.raw_text:
The rivalry between Spokane
over the possession of a certain
piece of property located 10 miles
east of the city on the Spokane river
has resulted in the Spokane men win
ming out.


The land is valuable owing to the
fact that it fronts on the river at a
point where the water power is at
its best and over which the new COM
pany has secured control The COM
pany has purchased 90 acres upon
one side of the stream and 20 acres
upon the other side, giving a COM
plete monopoly of the magnificent
power


The Spokane and Inland Electric
railway company proposed route
runs through a portion of the 80
acres.


The promoters Of the Spokane
company are John Tilsley, w. II.
Peck and s. G. Bergman. Mr. Peck
Is new conner, having arrived from
the east only last fall. His faith in
Spokane prosperity led him to invest
in It's real estate and he purchased a
home. He was for 10 years purchas-
ing agent for Forpaughs circus and
is man of means. Mr.. Tilsley and
Mr.. Bergman are Spokane men.


The main object of the company
Is to erect plant for the manufac-
ture of pressed brick, tile, etc. 1t
